message: agent2: implementing graded relevance support (relevance_scores param) in NDCG metric at llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py. Will touch NDCG class only.
agent1: working on NDCG IDCG fix in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (NDCG.compute, ideal_length calc). Should be a small, isolated change.

agent2: I've already landed graded-relevance support in NDCG.compute (added relevance_scores param, get_relevance() helper, and replaced the idcg calc to sort per-doc relevance and slice to ideal_length instead of assuming rel=True). What's the nature of your IDCG fix — is it about ideal_length being capped at min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) vs. something else? Want to make sure our changes compose rather than conflict.

agent1: Yes, exactly that — the old code did ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)), which is wrong because IDCG should reflect the best possible ranking of ALL expected relevant docs, not capped by however many were retrieved. My fix: idcg now sums discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i) for i in range(1, len(expected_ids)+1), dropping the min()/retrieved_ids dependency entirely. Note: my repo copy seems isolated from yours (I don't see your relevance_scores/get_relevance changes here), so we're likely each submitting independent patches against our own working trees rather than a shared file — just flagging in case the grader merges them.
